pattern: Since a user's online status only ever matters at the exact instant a message is being counted, replaying every event in timestamp order -- resolving status changes before any message that shares that same instant -- turns the whole scenario into a straightforward simulation over a per-user offline-until clock.
hints: Sort all events by timestamp first, and whenever a status-change event and a message event share the same timestamp, apply the status change first -- that ordering is what the scenario guarantees.
Track, for each user, the timestamp at which they become online again after going offline; a user is online at time t exactly when that stored timestamp is at or before t.

An 'ALL' message reaches every user regardless of status, a 'HERE' message reaches only users who are online at that exact moment, and explicit id mentions always count even for an offline user -- and the same id can appear more than once in a single message, with each occurrence counted separately.

edge_inputs.description: a user goes offline and comes back before being mentioned with HERE
an ALL message reaches an offline user too
the same user id mentioned twice in one message
a HERE message at the exact instant a user's offline period ends, so they are already back online
a HERE message one tick before a user's offline period ends, so they are still offline
events supplied out of timestamp order in the input array, requiring a stable sort before simulating
a user goes offline, comes back, and goes offline again later in the same run
a HERE message when every user happens to be offline

stress.description: random mixes of OFFLINE and MESSAGE events across a handful of users
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num_users = rng.randint(1, 20)
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    online = [True] * num_users
    events = []
    t = 1
    for _ in range(n):
        t += rng.randint(1, 50)
        if rng.random() < 0.3:
            candidates = [i for i in range(num_users) if online[i]]
            if candidates:
                uid = rng.choice(candidates)
                online[uid] = False
                events.append(["OFFLINE", str(t), str(uid)])
                continue
        kind = rng.choice(["ALL", "HERE", "IDS"])
        if kind == "ALL":
            events.append(["MESSAGE", str(t), "ALL"])
        elif kind == "HERE":
            events.append(["MESSAGE", str(t), "HERE"])
        else:
            tokens = ["id" + str(rng.randint(0, num_users - 1)) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 5))]
            events.append(["MESSAGE", str(t), " ".join(tokens)])
    return [num_users, events]
